question: Design a C++ class named `Book` with private attributes for `title` (string), `author` (string), and `isbn` (string). Include public methods `setTitle`, `setAuthor`, `setISBN` to set these values, and `displayBookInfo` to print all book details. Create an object of `Book` and demonstrate its usage.

test_cases.expected:
Title: The Hobbit
Author: J.R.R. Tolkien
ISBN: 978-0345339683

errors: The setter methods (setTitle, setAuthor, setISBN) contain a semantic error. Instead of assigning the input parameter to the corresponding private member variable (e.g., `this->title = newTitle;`), they declare new local variables with the same name as the member variable and assign the input to these local variables. As a result, the private member variables of the Book object remain uninitialized or default-constructed, leading to incorrect book information being displayed.
